Tags.RawText: @param[out] defaultConfig The configuration object containing default settings for adding a device.

Lines.FullText: The default config object is organized to always have 3 object-type properties:

Elements.RawText: The default config object is organized to always have 3 object-type properties:

Lines.FullText: - "General" Contains general properties such as "AutomaticallyConnectStreaming"

Elements.RawText: - "General" Contains general properties such as "AutomaticallyConnectStreaming"

Lines.FullText: - "Device": Contains a child object-type property for each available device type, with the key of each property

Elements.RawText: - "Device": Contains a child object-type property for each available device type, with the key of each property

Lines.FullText: being the ID of the device type. These can be configured to customize the `addDevice` call when using

Elements.RawText: being the ID of the device type. These can be configured to customize the `addDevice` call when using

Lines.FullText: connecting to the selected device type (eg. via the native or OPC UA protocols).

Elements.RawText: connecting to the selected device type (eg. via the native or OPC UA protocols).

Lines.FullText: - "Streaming": Same as device, but used to configure each individual streaming connection established

Elements.RawText: - "Streaming": Same as device, but used to configure each individual streaming connection established

Lines.FullText: when calling `addDevice`.

Elements.RawText: when calling `addDevice`.

Description.RawText:
*
     * The default config object is organized to always have 3 object-type properties:
     *   - "General" Contains general properties such as "AutomaticallyConnectStreaming"
     *   - "Device": Contains a child object-type property for each available device type, with the key of each property
     *     being the ID of the device type. These can be configured to customize the `addDevice` call when using
     *     connecting to the selected device type (eg. via the native or OPC UA protocols).
     *   - "Streaming": Same as device, but used to configure each individual streaming connection established
     *     when calling `addDevice`.

Lines.FullText: are performed sequentially in the order specified by connectionArgs.

Elements.RawText: are performed sequentially in the order specified by connectionArgs.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Connects to multiple devices in parallel using the provided connection strings and returns the connected devices.
     * Each connection is established concurrently to improve performance when handling multiple devices. The additions, in turn,
     * are performed sequentially in the order specified by connectionArgs.

Lines.FullText: Represents an openDAQ device. The device contains a list of signals and physical channels.

Elements.RawText: Represents an openDAQ device. The device contains a list of signals and physical channels.

Lines.FullText: Some devices support adding function blocks, or connecting to devices. The list of available

Elements.RawText: Some devices support adding function blocks, or connecting to devices. The list of available

Lines.FullText: function blocks/devices can be obtained via the `getAvailable` functions, and added

Elements.RawText: function blocks/devices can be obtained via the `getAvailable` functions, and added

Lines.FullText: via the `add` functions.

Elements.RawText: via the `add` functions.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Represents an openDAQ device. The device contains a list of signals and physical channels.
 * Some devices support adding function blocks, or connecting to devices. The list of available
 * function blocks/devices can be obtained via the `getAvailable` functions, and added
 * via the `add` functions.

Lines.FullText: All devices also provide access to their Device information, containing metadata such as the

Elements.RawText: All devices also provide access to their Device information, containing metadata such as the

Lines.FullText: device's serial number, location... They can also be queried for their current domain values

Elements.RawText: device's serial number, location... They can also be queried for their current domain values

Lines.FullText: (time) through its device domain.

Elements.RawText: (time) through its device domain.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * All devices also provide access to their Device information, containing metadata such as the
 * device's serial number, location... They can also be queried for their current domain values
 * (time) through its device domain.

Lines.FullText: As each device is a property object, a device has access to all Property object methods, allowing

Elements.RawText: As each device is a property object, a device has access to all Property object methods, allowing

Lines.FullText: each device to expose a list of custom properties such as sample rate, scaling factor and many

Elements.RawText: each device to expose a list of custom properties such as sample rate, scaling factor and many

Lines.FullText: others. By default, openDAQ devices have the UserName and Location string Properties.

Elements.RawText: others. By default, openDAQ devices have the UserName and Location string Properties.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * As each device is a property object, a device has access to all Property object methods, allowing
 * each device to expose a list of custom properties such as sample rate, scaling factor and many
 * others. By default, openDAQ devices have the UserName and Location string Properties.

Lines.FullText: Devices can be split up into three different types, with each devices supporting one or more:

Elements.RawText: Devices can be split up into three different types, with each devices supporting one or more:

Lines.FullText: 1. Physical devices

Elements.RawText: 1. Physical devices

Lines.FullText: Physical devices provide access to physical channels. They measure real-world data and send

Elements.RawText: Physical devices provide access to physical channels. They measure real-world data and send

Lines.FullText: it via packets through output signals of channels. The list of channels can be obtained via

Elements.RawText: it via packets through output signals of channels. The list of channels can be obtained via

Lines.FullText: `getChannels` as a flat list.

Elements.RawText: `getChannels` as a flat list.

Lines.FullText: 2. Client devices

Elements.RawText: 2. Client devices

Lines.FullText: Client devices can connect to other devices via their supported connection protocol. openDAQ

Elements.RawText: Client devices can connect to other devices via their supported connection protocol. openDAQ

Lines.FullText: natively supports connecting to TMS devices via its openDAQ OpcUa Client Module. A list of available

Elements.RawText: natively supports connecting to TMS devices via its openDAQ OpcUa Client Module. A list of available

Lines.FullText: devices a client device can connect to can be obtained via `getAvailableDevices`. The

Elements.RawText: devices a client device can connect to can be obtained via `getAvailableDevices`. The

Lines.FullText: `addDevice` is used to connect to/add a device.

Elements.RawText: `addDevice` is used to connect to/add a device.

Lines.FullText: 3. Function block devices

Elements.RawText: 3. Function block devices

Lines.FullText: Function block devices provide a dictionary of available function block types that can be added to them

Elements.RawText: Function block devices provide a dictionary of available function block types that can be added to them

Lines.FullText: and configured. The calculation of function blocks is done on the device itself. The dictionary

Elements.RawText: and configured. The calculation of function blocks is done on the device itself. The dictionary

Lines.FullText: of available function block types can be obtained via `getAvailableFunctionBlockTypes`. They

Elements.RawText: of available function block types can be obtained via `getAvailableFunctionBlockTypes`. They

Lines.FullText: can then be added via `addFunctionBlock`.

Elements.RawText: can then be added via `addFunctionBlock`.

Description.RawText:
*
 * Devices can be split up into three different types, with each devices supporting one or more:
 * 1. Physical devices
 *    Physical devices provide access to physical channels. They measure real-world data and send
 *    it via packets through output signals of channels. The list of channels can be obtained via
 *    `getChannels` as a flat list.
 * 2. Client devices
 *    Client devices can connect to other devices via their supported connection protocol. openDAQ
 *    natively supports connecting to TMS devices via its openDAQ OpcUa Client Module. A list of available
 *    devices a client device can connect to can be obtained via `getAvailableDevices`. The
 *    `addDevice` is used to connect to/add a device.
 * 3. Function block devices
 *    Function block devices provide a dictionary of available function block types that can be added to them
 *    and configured. The calculation of function blocks is done on the device itself. The dictionary
 *    of available function block types can be obtained via `getAvailableFunctionBlockTypes`. They
 *    can then be added via `addFunctionBlock`.
